factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is stable, with slight decreases in cash flow from operations, but remains sufficient for operations and investments.

evidence.text: We generated $247.3 million and $251.2 million of cash flow from operations... a decrease of $3.9 million compared to the six months ended June 30, 2015.
evidence.subsection: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing, primarily due to investments in technology infrastructure to improve efficiency and support growth.

evidence.text: We used $44.0 million and $19.6 million for capital expenditures... primarily due to the purchase and development of software and the continued construction of a second data center.
evidence.subsection: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable, with the company maintaining compliance with financial covenants and managing debt levels effectively.

evidence.text: The outstanding balance on the revolving credit facility was $465.0 million as of June 30, 2016. We were in compliance with all of the credit facility's financial debt covenants as of June 30, 2016.
evidence.subsection: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are being managed through a combination of dividends and stock repurchases, with a focus on maintaining a balance between returning capital and reinvesting for growth.

evidence.text: We used $127.5 million and $114.5 million to pay cash dividends... The number of shares we repurchase, if any, during future periods will vary based on our cash position, potential uses of our cash, and market conditions.
evidence.subsection: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

factors.summary: The company faced challenges in pricing power, with lower rates charged to customers.

evidence.text: Total Transportation revenues decreased 8.0 percent to $2.9 billion in the second quarter of 2016 from $3.1 billion in the second quarter of 2015. The decrease was driven by lower rates charged to our customers, including fuel, partially offset by increased volumes in most of our transportation services.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2015

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments like LTL and Other Logistics Services.

evidence.text: Our LTL net revenues increased 9.0 percent to $99.8 million in the second quarter of 2016 from $91.5 million in the second quarter of 2015. [...] Other logistics services net revenues [...] increased 24.0 percent to $26.2 million in the second quarter of 2016 from $21.1 million in the second quarter of 2015 primarily from volume growth in managed services.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2015

factors.summary: End market momentum varies, with growth in LTL, Other Logistics Services, and Ocean transportation, but declines in Intermodal and Air transportation.

evidence.text: Our LTL net revenues increased 9.0 percent [...] due to increased volumes. [...] Other logistics services net revenues [...] increased 24.0 percent [...] primarily from volume growth in managed services. [...] Ocean transportation net revenues increased 1.7 percent [...] due to increased net revenue margin and volume growth with new customers. [...] Intermodal net revenues decreased 21.8 percent [...] due to the alternative lower cost truck market. [...] Air transportation net revenues increased 2.7 percent [...] due to increased net revenue margin and an increase in volumes, partially offset by pricing declines.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2015

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable with a slight increase in personnel expenses.

evidence.text: Personnel expenses increased 2.4 percent to $270.3 million in 2016 from $264.0 million in 2015. [...] The increase in personnel expense was less than the increase in average headcount due to decreased expenses related to incentive plans that are designed to keep expenses variable with changes in net revenues and profitability.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2015

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering measurable savings, particularly in transportation costs.

evidence.text: Total purchased transportation and related services decreased 10.0 percent in the second quarter of 2016 [...] due to lower cost of transportation, including fuel. [...] Our truckload transportation costs decreased approximately eight percent, excluding the estimated reduction in fuel costs.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2015

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving, particularly in transportation services.

evidence.text: Our Transportation net revenue margin increased to 19.3 percent in the second quarter of 2016 from 17.5 percent in the second quarter of 2015 primarily due to lower purchased transportation costs, including fuel. [...] Income from operations as a percentage of net revenues increased to 39.3 percent in the second quarter of 2016 from 39.2 percent in the second quarter of 2015.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2015

factors.summary: Management shows cautious confidence with initiatives to lower costs and improve margins.

evidence.text: We keep our business model as variable as possible to allow us to be flexible and adapt to changing economic and industry conditions. [...] Our effective income tax rate was 37.1 percent for the second quarter of 2016 and 38.5 percent for the second quarter of 2015. [...] Net income increased 4.3 percent to $143.1 million in the second quarter of 2016 from $137.2 million in the second quarter of 2015.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2015

factors.summary: Near-term risks include potential challenges in pricing and increased competition.

evidence.text: The risks that could cause actual results to differ materially from our current expectations include changes in economic conditions; changes in market demand and pressures on the pricing for our services; competition and growth rates within the third party logistics industry; [...] risks associated with the potential impacts of changes in government regulations; risks associated with the produce industry, including food safety and contamination issues; fuel price increases or decreases, or fuel shortages; cyber-security related risks; the impact of war on the economy; changes to our capital structure; and other risks and uncertainties detailed in our Annual and Quarterly Reports.
evidence.subsection: FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is focused on expanding its global logistics services and investing in technology.

evidence.text: In January 2015, we completed our acquisition of Freightquote.com, Inc. [...] The acquisition enhances and brings synergies to our LTL and truckload businesses, and expands our e-commerce capabilities. [...] Our network offices work together to complete transactions and collectively meet the needs of our customers. [...] As an example, during 2015, approximately 49 percent of our truckload shipments were shared transactions between offices. [...] We believe these awards are an effective tool for creating long-term ownership and alignment between employees and our shareholders.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices, economic conditions, competition, government regulations, and cyber-security risks.

evidence.text: Risks that could cause actual results to differ materially from our current expectations include changes in economic conditions; changes in market demand and pressures on the pricing for our services; competition and growth rates within the third party logistics industry; [...] risks associated with the potential impacts of changes in government regulations; risks associated with the produce industry, including food safety and contamination issues; fuel price increases or decreases, or fuel shortages; cyber-security related risks; the impact of war on the economy; changes to our capital structure; and other risks and uncertainties detailed in our Annual and Quarterly Reports.
evidence.subsection: FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

factors.summary: A specific catalyst is the acquisition of Freightquote.com, which has brought synergies to the LTL and truckload businesses.

evidence.text: In January 2015, we completed our acquisition of Freightquote.com, Inc. [...] The acquisition enhances and brings synergies to our LTL and truckload businesses, and expands our e-commerce capabilities.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

factors.summary: Market share is stable with a diverse customer base, though there is some concentration risk.

evidence.text: Our customer base is very diverse and unconcentrated. Our top 100 customers represented approximately 29 percent of our total revenues and approximately 29 percent of our net revenues in 2015. Our largest customer was approximately two percent of our total revenues in 2015.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are intense with pressures from new entrants and aggressive rivals, but also opportunities through differentiation and strategic acquisitions.

evidence.text: The risks that could cause actual results to differ materially from our current expectations include [...] competition and growth rates within the third party logistics industry; [...] We believe these awards are an effective tool for creating long-term ownership and alignment between employees and our shareholders. [...] In January 2015, we completed our acquisition of Freightquote.com, Inc. [...] The acquisition enhances and brings synergies to our LTL and truckload businesses, and expands our e-commerce capabilities.
evidence.subsection: FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include a global network, diverse customer base, and strategic acquisitions.

evidence.text: Our network offices are a competitive advantage. Building local customer and contract carrier relationships has been an important part of our success. [...] Our customer base is very diverse and unconcentrated. [...] In January 2015, we completed our acquisition of Freightquote.com, Inc. [...] The acquisition enhances and brings synergies to our LTL and truckload businesses, and expands our e-commerce capabilities.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

factors.summary: Customer concentration is low, with a diverse customer base, though the top 100 customers account for 29% of total revenues.

evidence.text: Our customer base is very diverse and unconcentrated. Our top 100 customers represented approximately 29 percent of our total revenues and approximately 29 percent of our net revenues in 2015. Our largest customer was approximately two percent of our total revenues in 2015.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel, are decreasing, which is improving margins. The decline in input costs is beneficial for margins.

evidence.text: The decrease was due to lower cost of transportation, including fuel... excluding the estimated impacts of the reduction in fuel costs.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2016 Compared to Six Months Ended June 30, 2015

evidence.text: Total purchased transportation and related services decreased 10.0 percent in the second quarter of 2016 [...] due to lower cost of transportation, including fuel.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2015

evidence.text: Our truckload transportation costs decreased approximately eight percent, excluding the estimated reduction in fuel costs.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2015

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth has shown mixed performance, with a slight increase in net revenues across several segments during the six months ended June 30, 2016, compared to the same period in 2015, but a deceleration in total revenues during the three months ended June 30, 2016, compared to the same quarter in 2015.

evidence.text: Net revenues. Total Transportation net revenues increased 4.5 percent to $1.1 billion... compared to the six months ended June 30, 2015.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2016 Compared to Six Months Ended June 30, 2015

evidence.text: Total revenues and direct costs. Our consolidated total revenues decreased 6.9 percent in the second quarter of 2016 compared to the second quarter of 2015.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2015

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across different segments and transportation services. While some areas, such as truckload net revenues, show growth due to decreased costs, including fuel, overall transportation revenues have decreased due to lower rates charged to customers.

evidence.text: Total Transportation net revenues increased 4.5 percent... primarily due to lower transportation costs, including fuel. Our truckload net revenues increased 2.9 percent... primarily due to decreased cost of transportation, including fuel.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2016 Compared to Six Months Ended June 30, 2015

evidence.text: Total Transportation revenues decreased 8.0 percent to $2.9 billion in the second quarter of 2016 from $3.1 billion in the second quarter of 2015. The decrease was driven by lower rates charged to our customers, including fuel, partially offset by increased volumes in most of our transportation services.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2015

factors.summary: Volumes are increasing across most transportation services, with notable growth in LTL and Other Logistics Services. The evidence consistently shows positive volume trends, particularly in LTL, which saw higher growth in the three-month comparison than in the six-month period.

evidence.text: Truckload volumes increased approximately three percent compared to the six months ended June 30, 2015. LTL volumes increased approximately nine percent compared to the six months ended June 30, 2015.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2016 Compared to Six Months Ended June 30, 2015

evidence.text: Truckload volumes increased three percent in the second quarter of 2016 compared to the second quarter of 2015. [...] LTL volumes increased approximately seven percent in the second quarter of 2016 compared to the second quarter of 2015. [...] Other logistics services net revenues [...] increased 24.0 percent [...] primarily from volume growth in managed services.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2015

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving and expanding, driven by lower transportation costs, including fuel, and operational efficiencies. The improvements are consistent across both the three-month and six-month periods.

evidence.text: Our Transportation net revenue margin increased to 19.5 percent... compared to 17.2 percent... primarily due to lower transportation costs, including fuel. Income from operations increased 5.3 percent... compared to the six months ended June 30, 2015.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2016 Compared to Six Months Ended June 30, 2015

evidence.text: Our Transportation net revenue margin increased to 19.3 percent in the second quarter of 2016 from 17.5 percent in the second quarter of 2015 primarily due to lower purchased transportation costs, including fuel. [...] Income from operations as a percentage of net revenues increased to 39.3 percent in the second quarter of 2016 from 39.2 percent in the second quarter of 2015.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2016 , Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2015
